
```markdown
---
title: "La Liga: The Duopoly and Beyond"
subtitle: "How Barcelona and Real Madrid shaped Spanish football"
author: "Eric Schnitger"
date: 2026-04-27
categories: [data-visualization, python, sports-analytics, web-scraping]
image: thumbnail.png
description: |
 An interactive analysis of La Liga from 1992 to 2025, examining
 the Barca-Real duopoly, Atletico's rise, and the gap to relegation.
format:
 html:
   code-fold: true
   code-summary: "Show code"
   code-tools: true
   toc: true
   toc-depth: 3
execute:
 echo: true
 warning: false
 freeze: auto
---

## The Question

La Liga has been defined by two clubs for decades. But in the Messi/Ronaldo era,
they pushed each other to 90-100 point seasons while the bottom of the table
stagnated. Has the duopoly become more extreme over time?

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Summary

- **Real Madrid and Barcelona account for ~27 of 33 titles** since 1992
- **Atletico Madrid** (2014 title, consistent top-3) is the only structural challenge
- **Title-winning points surged in the Messi/Ronaldo era** (2008-2018), regularly hitting 90-100
- **The gap peaked around 2012-2016** before a slight correction
- **Individual TV deals** (pre-2016) directly funded the competitive imbalance
:::

```{python}
#| label: load-data-laliga
#| code-summary: "Import and load data"

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

from _utils import (
   LEAGUES, scrape_league, enrich_dataframe,
   build_league_chart, build_frequency_tables,
)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

config = LEAGUES["la_liga"]
df = scrape_league("la_liga", cache_dir=".")
df = enrich_dataframe(df)

print(f"Dataset: {len(df)} seasons loaded")
df[["Season", "Champion", "Title-Winning Points", "Survived Relegation",
   "Relegation Survival Points", "Relegated 1", "Relegated 2", "Relegated 3"]].tail(10)
```

## Interactive Analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-main-laliga
#| fig-cap: "La Liga: Title-winning vs. relegation survival points"
#| code-summary: "Build visualization"

eras = [
   {"label": "Full History (1992-2025)", "start": df["Season"].iloc[0],
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "La Liga: The Duopoly (1992-2025)"},
   {"label": "Classic Era (1992-2004)", "start": "1992/93", "end": "2003/04",
    "title": "La Liga: Classic Duopoly (1992-2004)"},
   {"label": "Messi/Ronaldo (2004-2018)", "start": "2004/05", "end": "2017/18",
    "title": "La Liga: The Messi/Ronaldo Era (2004-2018)"},
   {"label": "Post-Legends (2018-2025)", "start": "2018/19",
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "La Liga: Post-Legends (2018-2025)"},
]

outliers = [
   {"season": "2011/12", "y": 100, "text": "Real 100 pts",
    "color": "#FFD54F", "ax": 40, "ay": -25},
   {"season": "2013/14", "y": 90, "text": "Atletico title",
    "color": "#26C6DA", "ax": -50, "ay": -30},
]

# Resolve y-values from actual data where None
for outlier in outliers:
   if outlier["y"] is None:
       match = df[df["Season"] == outlier["season"]]
       if not match.empty:
           outlier["y"] = int(match["Title-Winning Points"].iloc[0])

fig = build_league_chart(df, config, eras=eras, outliers=outliers)
fig.show()
```

## Champions League Concentration

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top4-laliga
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequent La Liga Top 4 finishers"
#| code-summary: "Top 4 frequency"

top4_clubs, relegated_clubs = build_frequency_tables(df, config)
top4_clubs.head(10).style.format({"Percentage": "{:.1f}%"}).hide(axis="index")
```

## Relegated Clubs

```{python}
#| label: tbl-relegated-laliga
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequently relegated La Liga clubs"
#| code-summary: "Relegation frequency"

relegated_clubs.head(10).style.hide(axis="index")
```

## Context

- **Individual TV deals** until 2016/17 meant Real and Barca earned 5-10x more than bottom clubs
- **Collective bargaining** (2016+) has somewhat flattened revenue, but legacy advantages persist
- **Atletico's transformation** under Simeone (2011+) created a genuine third force
- No club outside the top 3 has won since Valencia in 2004

---

*Part of [The European Football Divide](../) project.*
```
